papers.title_en: Identifying the Unknown: Prompt-Free Open Vocabulary Anomaly Recognition for Robot-Object Interaction
papers.authors: Philipp Allgeuer, Jan-Gerrit Habekost, Stefan Wermter
papers.evidence: 开放世界环境中识别未知物体的异常识别方法
papers.tldr: 针对机器人在开放世界环境中识别未知物体的问题，本文提出AnomNOVIC框架，结合基于掩码自编码器的异常检测生成物体无关的候选框，再利用免提示开放词汇分类器进行识别，无需预定义候选类别。实验表明该方法能有效发现并识别先前未见物体，为开放世界自主系统提供了实用的视觉感知能力。
papers.abstract_en: Robots operating in real-world environments must in general be able to recognize previously unseen objects. As robotic systems move toward open-world autonomy, there is a growing, yet largely unmet, need for open vocabulary object detectors that are prompt-free and efficient enough for continuous deployment. We present AnomNOVIC, a two-stage known-workspace framework that combines a masked autoencoder (MAE) trained for anomaly detection, with NOVIC, a powerful real-time prompt-free open vocabulary image classifier. The MAE produces generic object-agnostic bounding boxes, allowing NOVIC to classify salient image regions without requiring a predefined candidate class list. We evaluate AnomNOVIC against strong open vocabulary baselines in a tabletop robot-object environment featuring the NICOL humanoid robot, reaching 47.1% AP / 57.5% AP50 for prompt-free recognition, and 59.0% AP / 72.5% AP50 if class candidates are provided. Across additional datasets, including an in-the-wild test set with 48 unique objects, AnomNOVIC reaches up to 82.6% prompt-free detection and classification accuracy. These results significantly surpass all tested open vocabulary baselines, including YOLO-World-v2, OWLv2, and YOLOE.
